title: Probability Mass Function vs. Probability Density Function
description: A visual comparison of discrete and continuous probability distributions, highlighting their key characteristics and how mathematical expectation is calculated for each.
relationships: A Discrete Random Variable uses a PMF which assigns probability to specific points. E[X] is calculated by summing (Σ) the product of values and their probabilities.
A Continuous Random Variable uses a PDF where probability is the area under the curve. E[X] is calculated by integrating (∫) the product of values and the PDF.
